ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) ProxyLogon-style w3wp.exe → shell child + ASPX webshell drop in Exchange virtual dirs
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM post-exploitation chain: an IIS Exchange worker (w3wp.exe under the MSExchange app pool) spawns cmd/powershell/cscript or drops an .aspx file into OWA/ECP/aspnet_client paths — the exact behaviour used to deploy China Chopper-style webshells after chaining CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065 (ProxyLogon).
ucs.splunk: (tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_hash) as hash values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" (Processes.parent_process="*MSExchange*" OR Processes.parent_process="*OWA*" OR Processes.parent_process="*ECP*") (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","ping.exe","hostname.exe")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | append [ tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_path values(Filesystem.process_name) as proc from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name="w3wp.exe" (Filesystem.file_path="*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\owa\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ClientAccess\\ecp\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\V15\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\ecp\\*") (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ashx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.asmx") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 7d;
let _exchange_drop_paths = dynamic([@"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\", @"\ClientAccess\ecp\auth\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\"]);
let _shell_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","ping.exe","hostname.exe","arp.exe","tasklist.exe"]);
let ProcChain = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MSExchange","OWA","ECP","DefaultAppPool")
    | where FileName in~ (_shell_children)
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
         or InitiatingProcessAccountName in~ ("system","network service","iusr")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              ChildImage = FileName,
              ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              SHA256, AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              EvidenceType = "shell_child_of_w3wp";
let AspxDrop = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".asmx"
    | where FolderPath has_any (_exchange_drop_paths)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              ChildImage = FileName,
              ChildCmd = FolderPath,
              SHA256, AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              EvidenceType = "aspx_drop_in_exchange_path";
union ProcChain, AspxDrop
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let lookback = 7d;
let _shell_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","ping.exe","hostname.exe","arp.exe","tasklist.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "w3wp.exe"
| extend ChildLeaf = tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])
| where tolower(ChildLeaf) in (_shell_children)
| where Account !endswith "$" or SubjectUserName in~ ("system","network service","iusr")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon HAFNIUM IIS Exchange Worker Spawning Shell Children
id: 8a4d2c9b-6e7f-4d3a-9b21-silk-typhoon-w3wp
status: experimental
description: Detects w3wp.exe (Exchange app pool) spawning cmd/powershell/recon LOLBins, the canonical post-exploitation behaviour after ProxyLogon (CVE-2021-26855 chain) used by HAFNIUM / Silk Typhoon to deploy webshells.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
    ParentCommandLine|contains:
      - 'MSExchange'
      - 'OWA'
      - 'ECP'
      - 'DefaultAppPool'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\hostname.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child
falsepositives:
  - Exchange administrative scripts run via OWA-side automation (rare)
  - Monitoring agents that intentionally exec from app-pool context
level: high
ucs.rationale: This is the textbook HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon kill-chain signature: Microsoft, Volexity and Mandiant all attributed the ProxyLogon mass-exploitation wave to this actor specifically because of the IIS-worker-spawns-shell + .aspx-webshell-in-aspnet_client pattern (China Chopper variants). Legitimate Exchange operations almost never have w3wp.exe parenting cmd/powershell/recon LOLBins, and almost never h
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon LSASS dump via comsvcs.dll/procdump on Exchange/edge servers
ucs.description: After web-shell foothold, Silk Typhoon characteristically credential-dumps LSASS using either Sysinternals procdump.exe or the rundll32 comsvcs.dll MiniDump trick, then stages the resulting .dmp into web-accessible Exchange paths or 7zips it for exfil. This UC pivots on the LSASS-target signal plus the actor's preferred dumping toolset and staging paths.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where ( (Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*" Processes.process="*MiniDump*") OR (Processes.process_name IN ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe") Processes.process="*lsass*") OR (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") (Processes.process="*MiniDumpWriteDump*" OR Processes.process="*Out-Minidump*")) OR (Processes.process_name="tasklist.exe" Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe") ) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 14d;
let _stage_paths = dynamic([@"\inetpub\wwwroot\", @"\HttpProxy\owa\auth\", @"\ClientAccess\ecp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\PerfLogs\"]);
let LsassDump = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
         or InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
         or InitiatingProcessAccountName in~ ("system","network service")
    | where (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
             and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll"
             and ProcessCommandLine has_cs "MiniDump")
         or (FileName in~ ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe")
             and tolower(ProcessCommandLine) has "lsass")
         or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
             and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("MiniDumpWriteDump","Out-Minidump","-MA System.Diagnostics.Process"))
         or (FileName =~ "tasklist.exe"
             and InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
              FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              SHA256, EvidenceType="lsass_dump_tool";
let StagedDmp = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed")
    | where (FileName endswith ".dmp" or FileName endswith ".7z" or FileName endswith ".zip")
    | where FolderPath has_any (_stage_paths)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("rundll32.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe","w3wp.exe","cmd.exe")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName=InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              FileName=tostring(FileName), ProcessCommandLine=InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              SHA256, EvidenceType=strcat("staged_artifact:",FolderPath);
union LsassDump, StagedDmp
| summarize Events=count(),
            FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp),
            Evidence=make_set(EvidenceType,10),
            Cmds=make_set(ProcessCommandLine,5)
            by DeviceName, AccountName
| where array_length(Evidence) >= 1
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let lookback = 14d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| extend ChildLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
         ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
         CmdLower = tolower(CommandLine)
| where (ChildLeaf == "rundll32.exe" and CmdLower has "comsvcs.dll" and CommandLine has_cs "MiniDump")
     or (ChildLeaf in ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe") and CmdLower has "lsass")
     or (ChildLeaf in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
         and (CmdLower has "minidumpwritedump" or CmdLower has "out-minidump"))
     or (ChildLeaf == "tasklist.exe" and ParentLeaf == "w3wp.exe")
| where Account !endswith "$" or ParentLeaf == "w3wp.exe" or SubjectUserName in~ ("system","network service")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon LSASS Credential Dump via comsvcs.dll or Procdump
id: 7d2c1f48-a31b-4e9a-ab66-silk-typhoon-lsass
status: experimental
description: Detects LSASS memory dumping techniques tied to Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM post-exploitation — comsvcs.dll MiniDump, procdump targeting lsass, or PowerShell MiniDumpWriteDump.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.001
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  comsvcs:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'comsvcs.dll'
    CommandLine|contains: 'MiniDump'
  procdump:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\procdump.exe'
      - '\procdump64.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'lsass'
  powershell_dump:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'MiniDumpWriteDump'
      - 'Out-Minidump'
  condition: comsvcs or procdump or powershell_dump
falsepositives:
  - Approved IR / forensic captures by SOC
  - Microsoft support engagements requesting LSASS dumps
level: high
ucs.rationale: Microsoft (MSTIC) and Volexity reporting on HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon Exchange compromises specifically called out procdump.exe LSASS dumps and the rundll32 comsvcs.dll MiniDump variant, followed by 7-Zip staging into web-accessible Exchange directories for exfil over the same web-shell channel. The combination of LSASS-targeting dump tooling AND staging in `\inetpub\wwwroot` / `\HttpProxy\owa\auth\` /
